source: Self-Transfer & C2C Scenario Pack (illustrative, non-exhaustive) · Mal-CRAM-US-01 v2.3 (CRAM-US-2026-07-FREEZE-03) · MAL-TA-01 v1.0 · MSB Compliance Risk Typology Register (Draft v0.1) · MAL-TM-PPC-CATALOG v1.0
oneLiner: Residence must be permitted (gate). Corridor sets the heat. Nationality & birthplace decide how hard we screen (flags, not blocks — unless combined with a money/residence/corridor nexus). Segment tells you how the account is likely to be abused by third parties — monitor the behaviour, never deny the person (residency-primary / non-discrimination / UDAAP).

corridors.label: US → Bangladesh
dominantTypologies: Remittance velocity / funnel to single beneficiary

onwardTraps.note: The register's hottest route (L×I 25). Self-transfers / C2C chaining onward to PK inherit enhanced-everything: mandatory EDD, tightest limits, reduced velocity, ≤6–12-month review, low anomaly tolerance.
Any nexus onward to Iran or a sanctioned wallet is a Prohibited override — block + OFAC blocking report within 10 business days (CRAM §7.1).

selfC2cFlows.coreTypologies: Name-mismatch at receiving side (CoP fail); 'own account' used to break the audit trail before dispersal; self-transfer velocity feeding a layering pre-stage
selfC2cFlows.hardControl: Same-name Confirmation of Payee (CoP) — hard control, not evidence; mismatch = block / review

selfC2cFlows.coreTypologies: Many-to-one funnel; remittance ≫ wage; hawala / hundi interface; missing settlement leg
selfC2cFlows.hardControl: Beneficiary coherence (name–ID–account, relationship, purpose); income evidence for remittance-vs-wage

selfC2cFlows.coreTypologies: High-frequency 'gifts' as layering cover; gifts from recent unknown counterparties (scam / mule)
selfC2cFlows.hardControl: Frequency cap for gifts; counterparty coherence

selfC2cFlows.coreTypologies: Serial 'loans' = unlicensed lending; loan-back round-trips; repayment with no visible prior loan
selfC2cFlows.hardControl: Relationship + expected-repayment declaration; pairing test with prior inbound; unlicensed-lending frequency rule

selfC2cFlows.coreTypologies: Structured many-to-one collections; high-frequency 'purchases' = undeclared business on a personal account
Crowd-collection into one individual (unlicensed fundraising / TF); high-risk corridor destinations
selfC2cFlows.hardControl: Low per-txn cap; aggregation review; corridor restrictions; unregistered / conflict-adjacent beneficiary → decline

periodicReviewLens.behaviour: Self-transfer name-mismatch at the receiving side (CoP fail)
periodicReviewLens.action: Block / review the transfer; investigate layering pre-stage

periodicReviewLens.behaviour: Self-transfer velocity spike, no lifestyle rationale
C2C inbound from many unrelated senders → rapid forward

periodicReviewLens.action: EDD; RFI on SoF; funnel check

periodicReviewLens.signals: Layering cover / undeclared business / unlicensed lending
periodicReviewLens.action: Re-classify; step-up; possible SME re-book or decline

periodicReviewLens.behaviour: Charity-to-individual fan-in then onward to high-risk zone
fiat↔crypto bursts / self-transfer to wallet then off-ramp
periodicReviewLens.action: Chain-analysis; mixer / sanctioned-wallet screen; High floor
KRI review; free-text keyword scan; step-up

periodicReviewLens.behaviour: New corridor with no profile rationale
Same customer using multiple MSBs / wallets for one route

rescoreCadence: Watchlist hit → SDN delta re-screen ≤24h, other lists ≤48h, re-score on hit. Alert triage: P1 ≤4h · P2 ≤24h · P3 ≤48h. High / PEP / Rule-1 relationships get a ≤12-month review (≤6-month on the hottest UAE→PK route). Any confirmed typology → most-restrictive rating persists until cleared.
